name: Reactive Barrier
effect: Automatically creates a barrier when you are hit by any form of crowd control.
when_to_buy: Early in the laning phase against teams with heavy CC.

name: Debuff Reducer
effect: Reduces the duration of stuns, slows, and CC-applying ultimates.
when_to_buy: When you are consistently stun-locked or stuck in crowd control during fights.
notes: Builds into Debuff Remover, a powerful active item covered in the Active Items section.

name: Spirit Shielding
effect: Significantly increases survivability against spirit damage.
when_to_buy: Early game against double-spirit lanes or spirit-heavy team compositions.

name: Spirit Resilience
effect: Stat-based passives that increase resistance to spirit damage or bullet damage, respectively.
when_to_buy: Mid-to-late game, after your core damage items are online. Press Tab to check whether the enemy team deals primarily spirit or weapon damage, then buy accordingly.

name: Bullet Resilience
effect: Stat-based passives that increase resistance to spirit damage or bullet damage, respectively.
when_to_buy: Mid-to-late game, after your core damage items are online. Press Tab to check whether the enemy team deals primarily spirit or weapon damage, then buy accordingly.

name: Spellbreaker
effect: Provides high spirit resist and debuff resist in addition to reduced spirit burst damage.
when_to_buy: Whenever you need the strongest spirit resistance available. Best-in-class for that purpose.

name: Counter Spell
effect: Transforms your parry into a full ability/item deflect. On a correctly timed parry, you negate the damage and effects of the incoming enemy ability or item.
when_to_buy: Every game, on any hero. Widely considered the single most impactful counter item in the game.
notes: Counter Spelling Bebop's bomb prevents the bomb placement and the follow-up uppercut, and puts the bomb on cooldown. However, Bebop still gains a soul stack from the interaction, which can make him a threat later in the game.

name: Knockdown
effect: Drops an anvil on a target, knocking them down and stunning them. Forces airborne or channeling heroes to the ground.
when_to_buy: Against flying heroes or heroes with long-animation ultimates.
notes: Effective against Mo & Krill when they are burrowing — Knockdown them, then secure the kill.

name: Slowing Hex
effect: Massively reduces enemy movement speed and silences movement-based abilities and items for over 3 seconds.
when_to_buy: Nearly every game — being slowed is extremely punishing and makes targets easy to pick off.
notes: Warp Stone — provides a quick directional teleport plus some bullet resist.

name: Disarming Hex
effect: Disarms the target for 4 seconds and reduces their bullet resist.
when_to_buy: When the enemy team is running mostly weapon/gun-damage builds. Escaping or killing enemies who rely on weapon damage.

name: Silence Wave
effect: Launches a projectile forward that suppresses (silences) everyone in its path.
when_to_buy: Against ability-reliant heroes, especially those who depend on an ability at low health.

name: Focus Lens
effect: Passively increases fire rate by 16%. On use, silences a target; 30% of all damage dealt during the silence is applied as bonus damage when the silence ends.
when_to_buy: As a point-and-click silence alternative to Silence Wave.

name: Unstoppable
effect: An automatic Counter Spell that lasts 5.5 seconds, deflecting all crowd control during that window.
when_to_buy: When you need prolonged CC immunity rather than single-instance parry protection.

name: Curse
effect: Silences, disarms, and prevents item usage on the target. Also interrupts channeled abilities.
when_to_buy: Against fed (high-economy) enemy carries. Best single-target shutdown item in the game.

name: Debuff Remover
effect: Purges all non-ultimate negative effects currently applied to you. Upgraded version of Debuff Reducer.
when_to_buy: Priority purchase against teams running multiple debuff-applying items or abilities (e.g., Slowing Hex, Knockdown).
notes: Cleanses all the active-item debuffs listed in this section.

name: Healbane
effect: 35% healing reduction. Heals you for 275 on kills.
when_to_buy: Early game, in any lane where the opponent heals constantly, or for heals in teamfights or close engagements.

name: Toxic Bullets
effect: 30% healing reduction. Applies bleed damage that drains a percentage of the target's max health over time.
when_to_buy: Against high-HP, beefy heroes. Although it has 5% less healing reduction than Healbane, the max-health bleed makes it superior into tanks.

name: Inhibitor
effect: 35% healing reduction. Reduces the target's outgoing damage by 35%, plus passive stat buffs.
when_to_buy: When an enemy hero is dealing high damage while also healing significantly.

name: Spirit Burn
effect: 70% healing reduction. Deals significant spirit damage and applies a burn.
when_to_buy: Against heroes that primarily build spirit damage, such as Mirage. Provides double the healing reduction of Healbane with substantial damage on top.

name: Crippling Headshot
effect: 30% healing reduction. On headshot, reduces the target's bullet resist and spirit resist by 18% for 12 seconds.
when_to_buy: Weapon-build carries looking to shut down a fed sustain hero, particularly Viscous. The resist shred amplifies your entire team's damage.

name: Rebuttal
effect: On a successful parry against an enemy hero, you heal, return the damage, and gain a 30% bonus damage buff for 6 seconds.
when_to_buy: When you are confident in your parry timing. A landed parry often leads directly to a kill.
notes: You can parry Viscous's Puddle Punch, returning the damage back to Viscous.

name: Decay
effect: Deals bleed damage based on the target's current health, plus a 40% healing reduction for 10 seconds.

name: Enduring Speed
effect: Grants movement speed and 30% slow resistance. Useful against heroes like Kelvin who apply frequent slows.

name: Close Quarters
effect: Grants 20% melee resist and 20% bonus weapon damage at close range. Buy in melee-heavy lanes.

name: Capacitor
effect: Removes non-ultimate buffs from the target. Strips away buffs like Seven's Power Surge, Page's shields, and Viscous's cubes.

name: Divine Barrier
effect: Can be used on teammates and cleanses ultimates such as Drifter's and Pocket's.

name: Suppressor
effect: Reduces enemy fire rate by 30%. Direct counter to weapon carries that depend on fire rate.

name: Return Fire
effect: Grants a chance to fire bullets back at the attacker. Effective against heroes like Haze.
notes: Type: Active | Builds into Metal Skin, which grants full bullet immunity for 5 seconds.

name: Rescue Beam
effect: Saves teammates from many of the threats described throughout this guide. Priority pickup for support-oriented builds.

name: Battle Vest
effect: Cheaper alternative to Bullet Resilience. Also provides fire rate. Suitable against gun-damage heroes like Haze.
when_to_buy: Budget option against weapon/gun-damage heavy teams.

name: Plated Armor
effect: Double the price of Battle Vest, but deflects bullets and on-hit effects. Effective against heroes like Infernus who build up burn stacks.
when_to_buy: When you need strong bullet protection and on-hit effect deflection.

name: Warp Stone
effect: Provides a quick directional teleport plus some bullet resist.
when_to_buy: When your goal is to escape or outplay with movement.

name: Metal Skin
effect: Upgraded Return Fire. Grants full bullet immunity for 5 seconds.
when_to_buy: Against heavy weapon-damage teams. Upgrade path from Return Fire.
